member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.DiagnosticIds.DiagnosticIds
member.summary: Various diagnostic IDs reported by this repo.
member.remarks: When adding a new diagnostic ID, add a corresponding suppression to the root Directory.Build.targets file, and add a documentation entry to docs/list-of-diagnostics.md.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.DiagnosticIds.DiagnosticIds.Experiments
member.summary: Experiments supported by this repo.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw
member.summary: Defines static methods used to throw exceptions.
member.remarks: The main purpose is to reduce code size, improve performance, and standardize exception messages.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNull``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified argument is .
member.typeparam: Argument type to be checked for .
member.param: Object to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrMemberNull``2(``0,``1,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified argument is , or if the specified member is .
member.typeparam: Argument type to be checked for .
Member type to be checked for .
member.param: Argument to be checked for .
Object member to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.
The name of the member.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfMemberNull``2(``0,``1,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified member is .
member.typeparam: Argument type.
Member type to be checked for .
member.param: Argument to which member belongs.
Object member to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.
The name of the member.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrWhitespace(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws either an or an if the specified string is or whitespace respectively.
member.param: String to be checked for or whitespace.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrEmpty(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the string is , or if it is empty.
member.param: String to be checked for or empty.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfBufferTooSmall(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the argument's buffer size is less than the required buffer size.
member.param: The actual buffer size.
The required buffer size.
The name of the parameter to be checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfOutOfRange``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the enum value is not valid.
member.param: The argument to evaluate.
The name of the parameter being checked.
member.typeparam: The type of the enumeration.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrEmpty``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the collection is , or if it is empty.
member.param: The collection to evaluate.
The name of the parameter being checked.
member.typeparam: The type of objects in the collection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentNullException(System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentNullException(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentOutOfRangeException(System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentOutOfRangeException(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentOutOfRangeException(System.String,System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
The value of the argument that caused this exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentException(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentException(System.String,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception.
member.remarks: If the is not a , the current exception is raised in a catch block that handles the inner exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.InvalidOperationException(System.String)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.InvalidOperationException(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: A message that describes the error.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfLessThan(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is less than min.
member.param: Number to be expected being less than min.
The number that must be less than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is greater than max.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is greater or equal than max.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is not in the specified range.
member.param: Number to be expected being greater or equal than max.
The lower bound of the allowed range of argument values.
The upper bound of the allowed range of argument values.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is equal to 0.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1
member.summary: Represents a span that's potentially created over a rented array.
member.typeparam: The type of objects held by the span.
member.remarks:
This type is used to implement a common pattern to improve performance when using temporary buffers. The pattern encourages the use of stack-based buffers when possible, which eliminate the overhead of garbage collection of buffers.
With this type, you make a speculative call to allocate a buffer. If the buffer is too large to fix on the stack, it is allocated from . If the buffer could be allocated on the stack, then no buffer is acquired from the array pool, and instead the caller is expected to use to get the buffer.

example.code: using var rental = new RentedSpan<char>(length); var span = rental.Rented ? rental.Span : stackalloc char[length];

member.name: F:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.MinimumRentalSpace
member.summary: The minimum size in bytes that triggers buffer rental.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: The desired length of the span. if this value is %lt;= 0, no buffer is allocated.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.Dispose
member.summary: Returns a rented array back to the array pool.
member.remarks:
If no array was actually allocated from the array pool (when is ), then this method has no effect.
Calling this method multiple times on the same object is not supported, don't do it.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.Span
member.summary: Gets a span over the rented buffer.
member.remarks: If no buffer was rented (because the buffer was deemed too small), then this returns an empty span. When a buffer isn't rented by this type, it's a cue to you to allocate buffer from the stack instead using stackalloc.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.RentedSpan`1.Rented
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether a buffer has been rented.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.NoopPooledObjectPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy that does nothing.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledCancellationTokenSourcePolicy
member.summary: An object pool policy for cancellation token sources.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledDictionaryPolicy`2
member.summary: An object pool policy for dictionaries.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledListPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy for lists.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledListWithCapacityPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy for lists with capacity.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PooledSetPolicy`1
member.summary: An object pool policy for sets.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory
member.summary: A factory of object pools.
member.remarks: This class makes it easy to create efficient object pools used to improve performance by reducing strain on the garbage collector.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreatePool``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool.
member.typeparam: The type of object to keep in the pool.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreatePool``1(Microsoft.Extensions.ObjectPool.IPooledObjectPolicy{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool with a custom policy.
member.typeparam: The type of object to keep in the pool.
member.param: The custom policy that is responsible for creating new objects and preparing objects to be added to the pool.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateResettingPool``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool for resettable objects.
member.typeparam: The type of object to keep in the pool.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.
member.remarks: Objects are systematically reset before being added to the pool.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateStringBuilderPool(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a pool of instances.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.
The maximum capacity of the string builders to keep in the pool. This defaults to 64K.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateListPool``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.typeparam: The type of object held by the lists.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateListPoolWithCapacity``1(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances, each with provided .
member.typeparam: The type of object held by the lists.
member.param: The capacity of each created instance.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateDictionaryPool``2(System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.typeparam: The type of the dictionary keys.
The type of the dictionary values.
member.param: Optional key comparer used by the dictionaries.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateHashSetPool``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.typeparam: The type of objects held in the sets.
member.param: Optional key comparer used by the sets.
The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.CreateCancellationTokenSourcePool(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates an object pool of instances.
member.param: The maximum number of items to keep in the pool. This defaults to 1024. This value is a recommendation, the pool may keep more objects than this.
member.remarks: On .NET 6 and above, cancellation token sources are reusable and this pool leverages this feature. When running on older frameworks, this pool is actually a no-op, every time a source is fetched from the pool, it is always a new instance. In that case, returning an object to the pool merely disposes it.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Pools.PoolFactory.SharedStringBuilderPool
member.summary: Gets the shared pool of instances.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Text.NumericExtensions
member.summary: Utilities to augment the basic numeric types.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Text.NumericExtensions.ToInvariantString(System.Int32)
member.summary: Formats an integer as an invariant string.
member.param: The value to format as a string.
member.returns: The string representation of the integer value.
member.remarks: This works identically to value.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture) except that it is faster as it maintains preformatted strings for common integer values.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Text.NumericExtensions.ToInvariantString(System.Int64)
member.summary: Formats a 64-bit integer as an invariant string.
member.param: The value to format as a string.
member.returns: The string representation of the integer value.
member.remarks: This works identically to value.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture) except that it is faster as it maintains preformatted strings for common integer values.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Memoization.Memoize
member.summary: Given a function of arity N (1 <= N <= 2), return a function that behaves identically, except that repeated invocations with the same parameters return a cached value rather than redoing the computation.
member.remarks: Memoize is like a , but for functions instead of values. Memoize will use the equality of the types of the input parameters. This means that arbitrary objects will use reference equality, unless those types define their own equality semantics. This implies that callers should take care to use types with Memoize that would be safe to put in a dictionary: if the type is mutable, and its Equals/GetHashCode depends on those mutable parts, unexpected behaviour may result.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Memoization.Memoize.Function``2(System.Func{``0,``1})
member.summary: Returns a function that remembers the results of previous invocations of the given function.
member.typeparam: The function input type.
The function output type.
member.param: The function that needs to be memoized.
member.returns: A function that appears identical to the original function, but duplicate invocations are nearly instant.
member.remarks: Computed values consume memory. Garbage collection will free up that memory when the returned Func is freed. If you're computing values for large numbers of inputs, bear this in mind: if the Func lives for a long time, memory usage can increase without bound.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Memoization.Memoize.Function``3(System.Func{``0,``1,``2})
member.summary: Returns a function that remembers the results of previous invocations of the given function.
member.typeparam: The type of the function's first parameter.
The type of the function's second parameter.
The function output type.
member.param: The function that needs to be memoized.
member.returns: A function that appears identical to the original function, but duplicate invocations are nearly instant.
member.remarks: Computed values consume memory. Garbage collection will free up that memory when the returned Func is freed. If you're computing values for large numbers of inputs, bear this in mind: if the Func lives for a long time, memory usage can increase without bound.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Memoization.MemoizedFunction`2
member.summary: Memoizer for functions of arity 1.
member.remarks: We don't use weak references because those can only wrap reference types, and we wish to support functions that return other kinds of values.
member.typeparam: Input parameter type for the memoized function.
Return type for the memoized function.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Memoization.MemoizedFunction`2.#ctor(System.Func{`0,`1})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The function whose results will be memoized.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Memoization.MemoizedFunction`3
member.summary: Memoizer for functions of arity 2.
member.typeparam: First input parameter type for the memoized function.
Second input parameter type for the memoized function.
Return type for the memoized function.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Memoization.MemoizedFunction`3.#ctor(System.Func{`0,`1,`2})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The function whose results will be memoized.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.LengthAttribute
member.summary: Specifies the minimum length of any or objects.
member.remarks: The standard supports only non generic or typed objects on .NET Framework, while type is supported only on .NET Core. See issue here . This attribute aims to allow validation of all these objects in a consistent manner across target frameworks.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.LengthAttribute.MinimumLength
member.summary: Gets the minimum allowed length of the collection or string.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.LengthAttribute.MaximumLength
member.summary: Gets the maximum allowed length of the collection or string.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.LengthAttribute.Exclusive
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the length validation should exclude the and values.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.LengthAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The minimum allowable length of array/string data. Value must be greater than or equal to zero.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.LengthAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The minimum allowable length of array/string data. Value must be greater than or equal to zero.
The maximum allowable length of array/string data. Value must be greater than or equal to zero.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.LengthAttribute.IsValid(System.Object,System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations.ValidationContext)
member.summary: Validates that a given value is in range.
member.remarks: This method returns true if the is . It is assumed the is used if the value may not be .
member.param: The value to validate.
Additional context for this validation.
member.returns: A value indicating success or failure.
member.exception: is less than zero or if it is greater than .
The validated type is not supported.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.TimeSpanAttribute
member.summary: Provides boundary validation for .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.TimeSpanAttribute.Minimum
member.summary: Gets the lower bound for time span.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.TimeSpanAttribute.Maximum
member.summary: Gets the upper bound for time span.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.TimeSpanAttribute.Exclusive
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the time span validation should exclude the minimum and maximum values.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.TimeSpanAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Minimum in milliseconds.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.TimeSpanAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Minimum in milliseconds.
Maximum in milliseconds.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.TimeSpanAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Minimum represented as time span string.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Minimum represented as time span string.
Maximum represented as time span string.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Data.Validation.TimeSpanAttribute.IsValid(System.Object,System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations.ValidationContext)
member.summary: Validates that a given value represents an in-range TimeSpan value.
member.param: The value to validate.
Additional context for this validation.
member.returns: A value indicating success or failure.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Buffering.SerializedLogRecord
member.summary: Represents a log record that has been serialized for purposes of buffering or similar.
Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: Logging severity level.
Event ID.
The time when the log record was first created.
The set of name/value pairs associated with the record.
An exception message for this record.
The formatted log message.
The approximate size in bytes of this instance.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Buffering.SerializedLogRecord.LogLevel
member.summary: Gets the record's logging severity level.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Buffering.SerializedLogRecord.EventId
member.summary: Gets the record's event ID.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Buffering.SerializedLogRecord.Timestamp
member.summary: Gets the time when the log record was first created.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Buffering.SerializedLogRecord.Attributes
member.summary: Gets the variable set of name/value pairs associated with the record.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Buffering.SerializedLogRecord.Exception
member.summary: Gets an exception string for this record.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Buffering.SerializedLogRecord.FormattedMessage
member.summary: Gets the formatted log message.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Buffering.SerializedLogRecord.SizeInBytes
member.summary: Gets the approximate size of the serialized log record in bytes.

member.summary: Add value to measure.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Latency.Internal.MeasureTracker.SetLong(Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Latency.MeasureToken,System.Int64)
member.summary: Set value of measure.
member.param: Token for measure.
Value of measure.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Latency.Internal.MeasureTracker.GetPositionOfMeasure(Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Latency.MeasureToken)
member.summary: Gets the position at which a measure has been added.
member.param: Token for the measure.
member.remarks: This function uses _measurePosition as a dictionary. The key is the order of the name in the registry. The value is the position in the tracking arrays, _measureNames and _measureValues.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Latency.Internal.MeasureTracker.Measures
member.summary: Gets the list of measures added.

member.summary: Gets the list of registered keys, ordered using default comparator.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Latency.Internal.Registry.KeyCount
member.summary: Gets the number of registered keys.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Latency.Internal.Registry.GetRegisteredKeyIndex(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the zero-based order of registered key.
member.param: The key to get order for.
member.returns: Index of key. Returns -1 if not registered.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Latency.LatencyContextOptions.ThrowOnUnregisteredNames
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether an exception is thrown when using unregistered names.
member.value: if the ILatencyContext APIs throws when using unregistered names. if it no-ops. The default value is .

member.summary: Represents a rule used for filtering log messages for purposes of log sampling and buffering.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.HttpRouteParameter
member.summary: Struct to hold metadata about a route parameter.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.HttpRouteParameter.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: parameter name.
parameter value.
conveys if the parameter value is redacted.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.HttpRouteParameter.Name
member.summary: Gets parameter name.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.HttpRouteParameter.Value
member.summary: Gets parameter value.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.HttpRouteParameter.IsRedacted
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the parameter value is redacted.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.IHttpRouteFormatter
member.summary: Http request route formatter.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.IHttpRouteFormatter.Format(System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.HttpRouteParameterRedactionMode,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Compliance.Classification.DataClassification})
member.summary: Format the http path using the route template with sensitive parameters redacted.
member.param: Http request route template.
Http request's absolute path.
Strategy to decide how parameters are redacted.
Dictionary of parameters with their data classification that needs to be redacted.
member.returns: Returns formatted path with sensitive parameter values redacted.

member.summary: Format the http path using the route template with sensitive parameters redacted.
member.param: Http request's route segments.
Http request's absolute path.
Strategy to decide how parameters are redacted.
Dictionary of parameters with their data classification that needs to be redacted.
member.returns: Returns formatted path with sensitive parameter values redacted.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.IHttpRouteParser
member.summary: Http request route parser.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.IHttpRouteParser.ParseRoute(System.String)
member.summary: Parses http route and breaks it into text and parameter segments.
member.param: Http request's route template.
member.returns: Returns text and parameter segments of route.

member.summary: Extract parameters values from the http request path.
member.param: Http request's absolute path.
Route segments containing text and parameter segments of the route.
Strategy to decide how parameters are redacted.
Dictionary of parameters with their data classification that needs to be redacted.
Output array where parameters will be stored. Caller must provide the array with enough capacity to hold all parameters in route segment.
member.returns: Returns true if parameters were extracted successfully, return false otherwise.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.ParsedRouteSegments
member.summary: Struct to hold the route segments created after parsing the route.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.ParsedRouteSegments.#ctor(System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.Segment[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: Route's template.
Array of segments.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.ParsedRouteSegments.RouteTemplate
member.summary: Gets the route template.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.ParsedRouteSegments.Segments
member.summary: Gets all segments of the route.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.ParsedRouteSegments.ParameterCount
member.summary: Gets the count of parameters in the route.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.Segment
member.summary: Struct to hold the metadata about a route's segment.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.Segment.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String,System.Boolean,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: Start index of the segment.
End index of the segment.
Actual content of the segment.
If the segment is a param.
Name of the parameter.
Default value of the parameter.
If the segment is a catch-all parameter.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.Segment.Start
member.summary: Gets start index of the segment.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.Segment.End
member.summary: Gets end index of the segment.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.Segment.Content
member.summary: Gets content of the segment.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.Segment.IsParam
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the segment is a parameter.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.Segment.ParamName
member.summary: Gets a name of the parameter.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.Segment.DefaultValue
member.summary: Gets a default value of the parameter.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Diagnostics.Segment.IsCatchAll
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the segment is a catch-all parameter.

member.summary: Extensions for common telemetry utilities.

member.summary: Adds http route processing elements.
member.param: object.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ExtendedLogger.EnrichmentTagCollector
member.summary: Used to collect tags in the modern logging path.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ExtendedLogger.EnrichmentTagCollector.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.Object}})
member.summary: Used to collect tags in the modern logging path.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ExtendedLogger.LegacyTagJoiner
member.summary: Used to collect tags in the legacy logging path.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ExtendedLogger.ModernTagJoiner
member.summary: Used to collect tags in the modern logging path.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ExtendedLoggerFactory.Config
member.summary: Gets the current config state that loggers should use.
member.remarks: This gets replaced whenever option monitors trigger. The loggers should sample this value and use it for an entire call to ILogger.Log so as to get a consistent view of config for the execution span of the function.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerFactoryScopeProvider
member.summary: Default implementation of

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.NullScope
member.summary: An empty scope without any logic

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.JustInTimeRedactor
member.summary: Performs delayed redaction in order to avoid allocating intermediate strings and enabling redacting from span to span when possible.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerEnrichmentOptions
member.summary: Options for logging enrichment features.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerEnrichmentOptions.CaptureStackTraces
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include stack traces when an exception is logged.
member.remarks: When set to and exceptions are logged, the logger will add exception stack trace with inner exception as a separate key-value pair with key 'stackTrace'. The maximum length of the column defaults to 4096 characters and can be modified by setting the property. The stack trace beyond the current limit will be truncated.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerEnrichmentOptions.UseFileInfoForStackTraces
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to consult debugging files (PDB files) when producing stack traces.
member.remarks:
Reading available debugging files produces richer stack traces, but can cost a substantial amount of time to generate. As a result, this option should only be turned on in development scenarios, not for production use.
This property has no effect if is .
This defaults to .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerEnrichmentOptions.IncludeExceptionMessage
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include the exception message when an exception is logged.
member.remarks: When both and are set to exception messages are also included in stack traces.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerEnrichmentOptions.MaxStackTraceLength
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum stack trace length to emit for a given log record.
member.remarks:
When set to a value less than 2 KB or greater than 32 KB, an exception will be thrown.
This property has no effect if is .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerEnrichmentOptionsValidator.Validate(System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerEnrichmentOptions)
member.summary: Validates a specific named options instance (or all when is ).
member.param: The name of the options instance being validated.
The options instance.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerRedactionOptions
member.summary: Options to control redaction.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerRedactionOptions.ApplyDiscriminator
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether a discriminator value is applied to each redacted value.
member.remarks: The discriminator is used to constrain the ability to correlate data between pieces of state. When this property is , the tag name of each value is included as part of the redacted text, generally making it impossible to correlate data between tags of different names.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggingEnrichmentExtensions
member.summary: Extensions for configuring logging enrichment features.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggingEnrichmentExtensions.EnableEnrichment(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggingBuilder)
member.summary: Enables enrichment functionality within the logging infrastructure.
member.param: The dependency injection container to add logging to.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggingEnrichmentExtensions.EnableEnrichment(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggingBuilder,System.Action{Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggerEnrichmentOptions})
member.summary: Enables enrichment functionality within the logging infrastructure.
member.param: The dependency injection container to add logging to.
Delegate the fine-tune the options.

member.summary: Enables enrichment functionality within the logging infrastructure.
member.param: The dependency injection container to add logging to.
Configuration section that contains .

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.SamplingLoggerBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Extensions for configuring logging sampling.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.SamplingLoggerBuilderExtensions.AddTraceBasedSampler(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggingBuilder)
member.summary: Adds Trace-based logging sampler to the logging infrastructure.
member.param: The dependency injection container to add logging to.
member.remarks: Sampling decisions for logs match exactly the sampling decisions for the underlying . You may want to configure Tracing Sampling separately as part of OpenTelemetry .NET.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.SamplingLoggerBuilderExtensions.AddRandomProbabilisticSampler(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggingBuilder,Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration)
member.summary: Adds Random Probabilistic logging sampler to the logging infrastructure.
member.param: The dependency injection container to add logging to.
The to add.
member.remarks: Matched logs will be randomly sampled according to the configured probability. Higher the probability value, higher is the probability of a given log record to be sampled in.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.SamplingLoggerBuilderExtensions.AddRandomProbabilisticSampler(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggingBuilder,System.Action{Microsoft.Extensions.Diagnostics.Sampling.RandomProbabilisticSamplerOptions})
member.summary: Adds Random Probabilistic logging sampler to the logging infrastructure.
member.param: The dependency injection container to add logging to.
The configuration delegate.
member.remarks: Matched logs will be randmoly sampled according to the configured probability. Higher the probability value, higher is the probability of a given log record to be sampled in.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.SamplingLoggerBuilderExtensions.AddRandomProbabilisticSampler(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggingBuilder,System.Double,System.Nullable{Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel})
member.summary: Adds Random Probabilistic logging sampler to the logging infrastructure.
member.param: The dependency injection container to add logging to.
Probability from 0.0 to 1.0.
The log level (and below) to apply the sampler to.
member.exception: is less than 0 or greater than 1.
member.remarks: Matched logs will be randomly sampled according to the configured . Higher the probability value, higher is the probability of a given log record to be sampled in.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.SamplingLoggerBuilderExtensions.AddSampler``1(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggingBuilder)
member.summary: Adds a logging sampler type to the logging infrastructure.
member.typeparam: Logging sampler type.
member.param: The dependency injection container to add logging to.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.SamplingLoggerBuilderExtensions.AddSampler(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggingBuilder,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LoggingSampler)
member.summary: Adds a logging sampler instance to the logging infrastructure.
member.param: The dependency injection container to add logging to.
The sampler instance to add.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.Binder.SourceGeneration.BindingExtensions.Get``1(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration)
member.summary: Attempts to bind the configuration instance to a new instance of type T.

member.summary: Registers a configuration instance which TOptions will bind against.

member.summary: Registers a configuration instance which TOptions will bind against.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.Binder.SourceGeneration.BindingExtensions.ValidateConfigurationKeys(System.Type,System.Lazy{System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.String}},Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.BinderOptions)
member.summary: If required by the binder options, validates that there are no unknown keys in the input configuration object.
